content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"william.white@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Hannah,\\n\\nI reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.\\n\\nPlease treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nI heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. I do not want those blended into one scope line.\\n\\nSecond, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.\\n\\nOnce the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.\\n\\nPlease turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-15T14:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close\", \"content\": \"Hi James,\\n\\nThank you for sending those specifics. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.\\n\\nBest,\\nMarina\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-08T19:08:23Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"william.white@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c\", \"content\": \"Hi William,\\n\\nI reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.\\n\\nNothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.\\n\\nThe FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.\\n\\nRyan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-21T20:05:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception\", \"content\": \"Hi Peter,\\n\\nFollowing up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.\\n\\nBest,\\nAnaya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-27T13:39:27Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nI have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.\\n\\nPlease leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [\"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nConfirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.\\n\\nNo substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJames\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:43:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

[{"email_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request", "content": "Hi Hannah,

I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

You may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.

Best,
William", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T20:45:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.

The CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.

Monday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-31T14:19:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition", "content": "Hi Owen,

I heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.

Their AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.

Given that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-29T21:30:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner review and sign-off", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I reviewed the Northstar Legal FY2025 working file and the Northstar Legal FY2025 Accounts Review Notes, and both are clean.

The ASC-710 partner-draw reclass is appropriate at the FY2025 amount of $18,750. Please proceed with the late post to FP-2025-12 for $18,750, reclassing the balance from 210000 to 211000.

Once the journal entry is posted and the updated balances are reflected in the final file, I will countersign the final deliverable.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-19T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close", "content": "Hi James,

Thank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.

Best,
Marina", "cc": [], "parent_id": "8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-08T19:08:23Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T20:05:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception", "content": "Hi Peter,

Following up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The Northstar item has already been shifted to the updated due date, and the remaining entry appears to be a documentation follow-up rather than an active deadline issue. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.

Best,
Anaya", "cc": [], "parent_id": "985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-27T13:39:27Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

I have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.

Please leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:45:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

Confirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

No substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.

Thanks,
James", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:43:00Z", "is_read": true}]

Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.\\n\\nCross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.\\n\\nProcess note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. Priya's read is that this should be a cancellation-event triggered AP void scan, not a periodic sweep, so a cancellation logged on the vendor side immediately fires an AP-side look at any open pre-bills for that engagement.\", \"priority\": 3, \"estimate\": null, \"due_date\": \"2025-10-03\", \"labels\": [\"northstar_legal\", \"ap-follow-up\", \"vendor-credit-memo\", \"documentation\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-30T14:56:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-30T14:56:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_0ab11dc60f2541ffa44cfb183c02c18a\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"in_progress\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up to existing CrownPeak follow-up record\", \"description\": \"Add the closeout summary for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 to the existing combined CrownPeak follow-up record so the three-of-three arc is documented in one place.\\n\\nContext:\\n- Manual review concluded this is not a duplicate or live billing dispute.\\n- The underlying Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer for northstar_legal was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when coverage moved in-house.\\n- Pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11, about eleven days after the cancellation was logged, and is now 81 days old.\\n- Recommended disposition is void as stale on Brookfield's side.\\n- CrownPeak AR confirmed a manual credit memo will be backfilled within five business days.\\n- Daniel Jones is taking the partner follow-up to Matthew Li; Priya Khatri is drafting the AP-side policy framing on cancellation-triggered void scans.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- Append the narrative disposition and source breadcrumbs from Slack/email to the existing CrownPeak follow-up record.\\n- Note that this closes the third of three CrownPeak items.\\n- Keep all three CrownPeak follow-ups consolidated on one record for future traceability.\", \"priority\": 3, \"estimate\": 2, \"due_date\": \"2025-10-03\", \"labels\": [\"northstar_legal\", \"ap-follow-up\", \"vendor-credit-memo\", \"documentation\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-30T13:51:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-30T13:51:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"linear_e91d48658876\", \"project_id\": null, \"team_id\": \"linear_7d4b3f1127cd\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"linear_4d9e2b202532\", \"state_id\": null, \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": 3115, \"title\": \"Managing partner update - major multi-service client win requires tax and compliance readiness checklist\", \"description\": \"Narrative event follow-up. Build a readiness checklist covering onboarding timing, tax document intake, annual report calendar setup, audit-support expectations, and staffing constraints through 2026-05 month-end. \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": null, \"state_id\": \"todo\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Review CrownPeak AP queue and tune duplicate detection for memo variance\", \"description\": \"Trigger invoice for northstar_legal: apinv_e0235997b4964a8f, CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-353041 for $459.84, has been sitting pending approval since 2026-03-09 (83 days old as of the weekly exceptions scan) and appears to be a duplicate of already paid sibling invoice VEN-030-353009, paid on 2026-03-13 for the same March 2026 MSSP cycle. [{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

Cross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.

Process note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. Priya's read is that this should be a cancellation-event triggered AP void scan, not a periodic sweep, so a cancellation logged on the vendor side immediately fires an AP-side look at any open pre-bills for that engagement.", "priority": 3, "estimate": null, "due_date": "2025-10-03", "labels": ["northstar_legal", "ap-follow-up", "vendor-credit-memo", "documentation"], "links": [], "is_archived": false, "created_at": "2026-05-30T14:56:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-30T14:56:00Z"}, {"id": "issue_0ab11dc60f2541ffa44cfb183c02c18a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "in_progress", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up to existing CrownPeak follow-up record", "description": "Add the closeout summary for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 to the existing combined CrownPeak follow-up record so the three-of-three arc is documented in one place.

Context:
- Manual review concluded this is not a duplicate or live billing dispute.
- The underlying Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer for northstar_legal was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when coverage moved in-house.
- Pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11, about eleven days after the cancellation was logged, and is now 81 days old.
- Recommended disposition is void as stale on Brookfield's side.
- CrownPeak AR confirmed a manual credit memo will be backfilled within five business days.
- Daniel Jones is taking the partner follow-up to Matthew Li; Priya Khatri is drafting the AP-side policy framing on cancellation-triggered void scans.

Acceptance criteria:

input.subject: Northstar AR outstanding + aging/staleness
input.description: Quantify AR balance on 110000, age it, and assess staleness/write-off risk (allowance, bad debt).

content.content: Task #1 created successfully: Northstar AR outstanding + aging/staleness

input.subject: WIP vs invoiced/approved tie-out
input.description: Check WIP 119000 balance ties to billable hours, invoiced and approved amounts; find unrecognized/blocked WIP JEs.

content.content: Task #2 created successfully: WIP vs invoiced/approved tie-out

input.subject: Scope vs engagement letter
input.description: Read engagement letter(s) in Records Vault; compare covered scope to actual work (accounting/advisory/tax). Identify out-of-scope work.

content.content: Task #3 created successfully: Scope vs engagement letter

input.subject: AP vendor invoices aging in queue
input.description: Review Northstar-linked AP invoices stuck in pending_approval (CrownPeak items); quantify aging.

content.content: Task #4 created successfully: AP vendor invoices aging in queue

task.subject: AP vendor invoices aging in queue

input.subject: Comms vs numbers reconciliation
input.description: Pull Slack/email re Northstar billing/scope/budget; check story matches GL/engagement records.

content.content: Task #5 created successfully: Comms vs numbers reconciliation

input.subject: Linear/Airtable open items
input.description: Identify open Linear issues and Airtable records touching Northstar not closed out.

content.content: Task #6 created successfully: Linear/Airtable open items

input.description: Compile summary; update/create Airtable engagement record; file to Records Vault w/ retention+classification; email George+Daniel.

- Propose a duplicate-detection rule adjustment or fuzzy-match threshold change for memo-variance cases like "Mar MSSP retainer" vs "March MSSP monthly fee" for Priya to sign off on as rule owner.
- Review the two other long-pending CrownPeak invoices for northstar_legal and summarize whether they are approval bottlenecks, coding issues, or separate vendor follow-ups.
- Capture recommended AP queue cleanup steps for CrownPeak items so the same pattern is easier to detect going forward.

Related context from internal escalation is preserved in email thread email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0003 and vendor confirmation email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0004.", "priority": 2, "estimate": 3, "due_date": "2026-06-02", "labels": ["ap-controls", "duplicate-detection", "vendor-review", "northstar_legal", "crownpeak"], "links": [], "is_archived": false, "created_at": "2026-05-20T13:23:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-27T18:44:07Z"}, {"id": "issue_7e7797e4560f4f07b0b936d4dff032bb", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "todo", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection follow-up", "description": "Matthew Li approved a narrative dismissal for apinv_e0235997b4964a8f based on Brenda Abbas's vendor confirmation that VEN-030-353041 was a February 2026 CrownPeak platform-migration reissue and the matched paid sibling invoice ID VEN-030-353009 (paid 2026-03-13 for the March 2026 MSSP cycle).

input.content:
# Northstar Legal — Engagement Status Review
**Prepared for:** Daniel Jones (Accounts Manager) ahead of partner discussion with Matthew Li
**Prepared by:** George McAdam (Accounts Senior)
**Date:** 12 June 2026
**Entity:** northstar_legal | **Latest closed period:** FP-2026-04 | **FP-2026-05:** OPEN (BD5 close 2026-06-05 already passed)
**Classification:** Restricted — internal engagement / partner-decision working paper

---

## 1. Bottom line
The Northstar engagement is carrying four overlapping problems that, taken together, support a change-order conversation with Matthew:

1. **Scope is undocumented.** The only engagement letter on file for Northstar is an **FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter** (executed 2026-05-11). The recurring **accounting / bookkeeping / monthly close / management-accounts / annual-accounts / tax-prep / advisory** work we are actually doing is **not covered by any engagement letter in Records Vault.**
2. **WIP does not tie out** to confirmed, approved billable hours and is unusually large (~$2.0M, ~73% of YTD revenue). The May close is unposted and three WIP-recognition entries (~$95.3K) are blocked pending an hours tie-out / client input.
3. **AR / allowance is distorted.** Gross AR is $647.4K but the Allowance for Doubtful Accounts is $1,057.7K — i.e. a **negative net receivable** — alongside $427.0K of YTD bad-debt expense. This is not a supportable position and needs clean-up.
4. **AP backlog is large and stale.** 87 Northstar vendor invoices (~$302K) sit in `pending_approval` with **no approver assigned**, the oldest ~340 days old (July 2025). The three CrownPeak items everyone has been discussing are still open in SAP despite being "resolved" in the comms.

The story in Slack/email only covers a sliver of this (mostly the three CrownPeak invoices). The numbers in the GL/SAP are materially worse and broader than the comms suggest.

---

## 2. AR — outstanding, aging, staleness risk
| Item | Account | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| Accounts Receivable – Client Billings | 110000 | **$647,359.49** |
| Allowance for Doubtful Accounts | 117000 | **$1,057,727.96 (cr)** |
| Bad Debt Expense (YTD) | 590000 | **$427,018.64** |
| YTD revenue (401000–406000) | — | ~$2,740,754 |

- **Allowance exceeds gross AR (164%).** Net AR is negative, which is not defensible. It points to either an over-provision or write-offs that were expensed but never relieved against the allowance. Either way this needs remediation **before the FY2026 audit** (and is a self-review concern — see §5).
- AR turns over reasonably each month (monthly realizations and client payments both in the $60K–$140K range), so the *operating* receivable is being collected; the problem is the **allowance/bad-debt clean-up**, not day-to-day collection.
- **Staleness risk:** the heavy, recurring bad-debt write-off activity (numerous write-off JEs across FY2025–FY2026) and the over-sized allowance indicate a backlog of old/uncollectible balances being written off rather than actively worked.
- *Data note:* invoice-level aging buckets (30/60/90/120+) are not derivable from the GL feed alone. If the partner wants precise buckets, pull the AR aging from the billing sub-ledger. The GL-level signals above are sufficient to flag the issue.

## 3. WIP vs invoiced / approved
| Item | Account | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| Work in Process – Unbilled Time | 119000 | **$2,013,942.79** (was $1,914,360.46 at 4/30) |

- WIP is **~73% of YTD revenue** and growing — a large book of recognised-but-unbilled work. Conversion of WIP into billings (AR) is lagging.
- **It does not currently tie out.** FP-2026-05 is still open (past its 2026-06-05 BD5 close) and the *entire* May close is unposted (30 JEs in draft/submitted, none approved/posted). Among them, **three WIP-recognition entries totalling ~$95,289** are sitting unposted:
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 — $34,495.06 (draft)
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0020 — $45,263.28 (submitted)
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0051 — $15,531.00 (submitted)
- This matches the Slack thread (≈19 May) in which the **draft WIP recognition JE was held pending the billable-hours tie-out and "pending client input."** So WIP is not reconciled to confirmed/approved hours, and the May tie-out is incomplete.
- **Recommendation:** complete a WIP→billings reconciliation (confirmed hours → recognised WIP → billed AR) before signing off May, and treat the ~$2.0M WIP as a realization-risk item for the partner.

## 4. AP — vendor invoices aging in queue
- **87 Northstar AP invoices in `pending_approval`, ~$302,063 total, none with an approver assigned.** Aging spans FP-2025-07 through FP-2026-05.
- **Oldest / notable aged items:**
  - VEN-030-817856 (CrownPeak) **$1,152.41**, invoice 2025-07-08 (~**340 days**) — mis-coded to 210000; review says re-code to 219000 and release; **still pending in SAP**.
  - VEN-018-339868 (BlueMesa) $10,998.09 (2025-07-23); VEN-001-551785 (LexHarbor) $9,863.97 (2025-07-22); VEN-027-392197 (Ridgewell) $8,547.33 (2025-07-25); VEN-017-208333 (MetroLink) $5,634.15; VEN-039-15742 (AdvoSphere) $5,147.22 — all ~320+ days.
  - VEN-021-709994 (VaultSpan) $12,896.12; VEN-025-944371 (Stonepath) $16,213.76; VEN-033-523270 (ArchiveSpring) $11,786.59; VEN-036-501397 (Cobalt Harbor) $10,866.53 — material items still open.
- **The three CrownPeak items everyone discussed are all still open in SAP** despite being treated as resolved in the comms (see §6):
  - VEN-030-736427 $422.32 — void approved by Matthew (2026-05-31) → **still `pending_approval`, not voided**.
  - VEN-030-353041 $459.84 — dismissed as a duplicate of paid VEN-030-353009 (Matthew narrative sign-off) → **still `pending_approval`, not voided**.
  - VEN-030-817856 $1,152.41 — re-code & release recommended → **still `pending_approval`, not re-coded/released**.
- **Takeaway:** this is a structural approval-routing gap (no approver assignment), not three one-offs. ~$302K is sitting unactioned and overstating open AP / understating cash discipline on the client's books.

## 5. Scope vs the engagement letter (the change-order question)
- **Only engagement letter on file:** *"Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter – Executed 2026-05-11"* (doc_0036f5b991574808; restricted; FIRM_INTERNAL). It is the **only `engagement_letter` document in Records Vault at all.** (The stored file is a 102-byte stub; full scope text could not be retrieved from the vault — flagged so the partner can confirm against the signed original.)
- **Work actually being performed for Northstar** (evidenced in GL/SAP/Vault/comms):
  - Monthly bookkeeping & close, IOLTA trust accounting, daily cash/recon work;
  - Management-accounts packages (e.g. FP-2026-04 package);
  - FY2025 annual-accounts / financial-statements working file (IOLTA + operating cash recs, fixed-asset rollforward, prepaid schedule);
  - FY2025 federal + multi-state **tax preparation** (Form 1065, K-1 allocations, M-1; SALT late-post $4,820.30; ASC-710 partner-draw reclass $18,750);
  - WIP recognition, AR/AP processing.
- **None of this recurring accounting/advisory/tax work is covered by a documented engagement letter.** The audit EL does not document it, and there is no separate accounting/advisory or tax engagement letter on file.
- **Independence / self-review flag:** Brookfield is both **keeping Northstar's books and performing the FY2026 audit** (audit EL 2026-05-11; audit materiality memo 2026-05-18). Doing the bookkeeping and the audit for the same client raises an AICPA independence/self-review question that the partner should confirm is covered by a safeguard — especially given the AR/allowance clean-up in §2.
- **Precedent for the mechanism:** the firm just raised an **Acme Cloud change order** (May 2026) for ~15 hours of engagement-letter overage, and that proposal explicitly referenced *"the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement."* So a change-order/scope-pricing mechanism already exists and has Northstar precedent.

**Conclusion for the partner meeting:** the WIP + AR picture and the breadth of recurring work strongly indicate we are delivering well beyond what the engagement records document, and it has **not** been captured as a change order or a current accounting/advisory engagement letter. Daniel's decision: **absorb**, or **go to Matthew for a change order + a proper accounting/advisory (and tax) engagement letter** to paper the scope.

## 6. Do the comms match the numbers?
- **AP / CrownPeak — NO.** Slack, email and Linear all read as "resolved" (void approved, duplicate dismissed, re-code & release). In SAP **all three CrownPeak invoices remain `pending_approval`, unchanged.** The comms also only ever discuss those 3 items — they are silent on the other 84 invoices / ~$300K and on the no-approver-assigned routing gap.
- **WIP — YES.** Comms say WIP recognition is on hold pending the hours tie-out/client input; the GL confirms it (3 unposted WIP JEs, May close not posted).
- **Scope — NO.** Comms show heavy accounting/tax/advisory plus the audit kicking off; the engagement records show only an audit EL. The work has outrun the documentation.

## 7. Open Linear / Airtable items touching Northstar
- **Linear (open):** 5 open Northstar items, all CrownPeak AP follow-ups —
  - `Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up` (in_progress)
  - `Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification` (in_progress)
  - `Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule` (in_progress)
  - `Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection` (todo)
  - `Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection` (todo)
  - (1 item `Close out CrownPeak three-item arc` is done.) **All are documentation-only; none have actually cleared the invoices in SAP.**
- **Airtable:** no Northstar-specific engagement record existed in either the Close Blocker Triage Log or AP Workflow Exceptions tables. An engagement-status record has now been created in the Close Blocker Triage Log to reflect this review.

## 8. Recommended next steps
1. **Scope / change order (partner call):** decide absorb vs. change order; if change order, paper a current **accounting/advisory + tax engagement letter** for Northstar and confirm the audit-independence safeguard.
2. **AP clean-up:** assign an approver and work the 87-item / ~$302K `pending_approval` queue; actually execute the three CrownPeak dispositions in SAP so the ledger matches the comms.
3. **WIP:** complete the billable-hours tie-out, post the May WIP entries, finish the (overdue) May close, and run a WIP→billings reconciliation.
4. **AR/allowance:** investigate the $1.06M allowance vs $0.65M gross AR; relieve write-offs against the allowance / right-size the provision before the FY2026 audit.

*Figures sourced from Oracle GL (balances as of FP-2026-04 close / current), SAP sub-ledger (AP & AR), Records Vault, Linear and Airtable on 2026-06-12.*

| Accounts Receivable – Client Billings | 110000 | **$647,359.49** |
| Allowance for Doubtful Accounts | 117000 | **$1,057,727.96 (cr)** |
| Bad Debt Expense (YTD) | 590000 | **$427,018.64** |
| YTD revenue (401000–406000) | — | ~$2,740,754 |

- **Allowance exceeds gross AR (164%).** Net AR is negative, which is not defensible. It points to either an over-provision or write-offs that were expensed but never relieved against the allowance. Either way this needs remediation **before the FY2026 audit** (and is a self-review concern — see §5).
- AR turns over reasonably each month (monthly realizations and client payments both in the $60K–$140K range), so the *operating* receivable is being collected; the problem is the **allowance/bad-debt clean-up**, not day-to-day collection.
- **Staleness risk:** the heavy, recurring bad-debt write-off activity (numerous write-off JEs across FY2025–FY2026) and the over-sized allowance indicate a backlog of old/uncollectible balances being written off rather than actively worked.
- *Data note:* invoice-level aging buckets (30/60/90/120+) are not derivable from the GL feed alone. If the partner wants precise buckets, pull the AR aging from the billing sub-ledger. The GL-level signals above are sufficient to flag the issue.

## 3. WIP vs invoiced / approved
| Item | Account | Balance |
|---|---|---|
| Work in Process – Unbilled Time | 119000 | **$2,013,942.79** (was $1,914,360.46 at 4/30) |

- WIP is **~73% of YTD revenue** and growing — a large book of recognised-but-unbilled work. Conversion of WIP into billings (AR) is lagging.
- **It does not currently tie out.** FP-2026-05 is still open (past its 2026-06-05 BD5 close) and the *entire* May close is unposted (30 JEs in draft/submitted, none approved/posted). Among them, **three WIP-recognition entries totalling ~$95,289** are sitting unposted:
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 — $34,495.06 (draft)
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0020 — $45,263.28 (submitted)
  - JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0051 — $15,531.00 (submitted)
- This matches the Slack thread (≈19 May) in which the **draft WIP recognition JE was held pending the billable-hours tie-out and "pending client input."** So WIP is not reconciled to confirmed/approved hours, and the May tie-out is incomplete.
- **Recommendation:** complete a WIP→billings reconciliation (confirmed hours → recognised WIP → billed AR) before signing off May, and treat the ~$2.0M WIP as a realization-risk item for the partner.

## 4. AP — vendor invoices aging in queue
- **87 Northstar AP invoices in `pending_approval`, ~$302,063 total, none with an approver assigned.** Aging spans FP-2025-07 through FP-2026-05.
- **Oldest / notable aged items:**
  - VEN-030-817856 (CrownPeak) **$1,152.41**, invoice 2025-07-08 (~**340 days**) — mis-coded to 210000; review says re-code to 219000 and release; **still pending in SAP**.
  - VEN-018-339868 (BlueMesa) $10,998.09 (2025-07-23); VEN-001-551785 (LexHarbor) $9,863.97 (2025-07-22); VEN-027-392197 (Ridgewell) $8,547.33 (2025-07-25); VEN-017-208333 (MetroLink) $5,634.15; VEN-039-15742 (AdvoSphere) $5,147.22 — all ~320+ days.
  - VEN-021-709994 (VaultSpan) $12,896.12; VEN-025-944371 (Stonepath) $16,213.76; VEN-033-523270 (ArchiveSpring) $11,786.59; VEN-036-501397 (Cobalt Harbor) $10,866.53 — material items still open.
- **The three CrownPeak items everyone discussed are all still open in SAP** despite being treated as resolved in the comms (see §6):
  - VEN-030-736427 $422.32 — void approved by Matthew (2026-05-31) → **still `pending_approval`, not voided**.
  - VEN-030-353041 $459.84 — dismissed as a duplicate of paid VEN-030-353009 (Matthew narrative sign-off) → **still `pending_approval`, not voided**.
  - VEN-030-817856 $1,152.41 — re-code & release recommended → **still `pending_approval`, not re-coded/released**.
- **Takeaway:** this is a structural approval-routing gap (no approver assignment), not three one-offs. ~$302K is sitting unactioned and overstating open AP / understating cash discipline on the client's books.

## 5. Scope vs the engagement letter (the change-order question)
- **Only engagement letter on file:** *"Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter – Executed 2026-05-11"* (doc_0036f5b991574808; restricted; FIRM_INTERNAL). It is the **only `engagement_letter` document in Records Vault at all.** (The stored file is a 102-byte stub; full scope text could not be retrieved from the vault — flagged so the partner can confirm against the signed original.)
- **Work actually being performed for Northstar** (evidenced in GL/SAP/Vault/comms):
  - Monthly bookkeeping & close, IOLTA trust accounting, daily cash/recon work;
  - Management-accounts packages (e.g. FP-2026-04 package);
  - FY2025 annual-accounts / financial-statements working file (IOLTA + operating cash recs, fixed-asset rollforward, prepaid schedule);
  - FY2025 federal + multi-state **tax preparation** (Form 1065, K-1 allocations, M-1; SALT late-post $4,820.30; ASC-710 partner-draw reclass $18,750);
  - WIP recognition, AR/AP processing.
- **None of this recurring accounting/advisory/tax work is covered by a documented engagement letter.** The audit EL does not document it, and there is no separate accounting/advisory or tax engagement letter on file.
- **Independence / self-review flag:** Brookfield is both **keeping Northstar's books and performing the FY2026 audit** (audit EL 2026-05-11; audit materiality memo 2026-05-18). Doing the bookkeeping and the audit for the same client raises an AICPA independence/self-review question that the partner should confirm is covered by a safeguard — especially given the AR/allowance clean-up in §2.
- **Precedent for the mechanism:** the firm just raised an **Acme Cloud change order** (May 2026) for ~15 hours of engagement-letter overage, and that proposal explicitly referenced *"the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement."* So a change-order/scope-pricing mechanism already exists and has Northstar precedent.

**Conclusion for the partner meeting:** the WIP + AR picture and the breadth of recurring work strongly indicate we are delivering well beyond what the engagement records document, and it has **not** been captured as a change order or a current accounting/advisory engagement letter. Daniel's decision: **absorb**, or **go to Matthew for a change order + a proper accounting/advisory (and tax) engagement letter** to paper the scope.

## 6. Do the comms match the numbers?
- **AP / CrownPeak — NO.** Slack, email and Linear all read as "resolved" (void approved, duplicate dismissed, re-code & release). In SAP **all three CrownPeak invoices remain `pending_approval`, unchanged.** The comms also only ever discuss those 3 items — they are silent on the other 84 invoices / ~$300K and on the no-approver-assigned routing gap.
- **WIP — YES.** Comms say WIP recognition is on hold pending the hours tie-out/client input; the GL confirms it (3 unposted WIP JEs, May close not posted).
- **Scope — NO.** Comms show heavy accounting/tax/advisory plus the audit kicking off; the engagement records show only an audit EL. The work has outrun the documentation.

## 7. Open Linear / Airtable items touching Northstar
- **Linear (open):** 5 open Northstar items, all CrownPeak AP follow-ups —
  - `Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up` (in_progress)
  - `Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification` (in_progress)
  - `Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule` (in_progress)
  - `Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection` (todo)
  - `Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection` (todo)
  - (1 item `Close out CrownPeak three-item arc` is done.) **All are documentation-only; none have actually cleared the invoices in SAP.**
- **Airtable:** no Northstar-specific engagement record existed in either the Close Blocker Triage Log or AP Workflow Exceptions tables. An engagement-status record has now been created in the Close Blocker Triage Log to reflect this review.

## 8. Recommended next steps
1. **Scope / change order (partner call):** decide absorb vs. change order; if change order, paper a current **accounting/advisory + tax engagement letter** for Northstar and confirm the audit-independence safeguard.
2. **AP clean-up:** assign an approver and work the 87-item / ~$302K `pending_approval` queue; actually execute the three CrownPeak dispositions in SAP so the ledger matches the comms.
3. **WIP:** complete the billable-hours tie-out, post the May WIP entries, finish the (overdue) May close, and run a WIP→billings reconciliation.
4. **AR/allowance:** investigate the $1.06M allowance vs $0.65M gross AR; relieve write-offs against the allowance / right-size the provision before the FY2026 audit.

*Figures sourced from Oracle GL (balances as of FP-2026-04 close / current), SAP sub-ledger (AP & AR), Records Vault, Linear and Airtable on 2026-06-12.*

input.subject: Northstar Legal — engagement status review (where things actually stand) for the Matthew discussion
input.content:
Daniel,

Here's the full picture on Northstar you asked for, pulled from the GL, SAP, Records Vault, Linear and Airtable as of 12 June. The detailed working paper is attached and filed to Records Vault (doc_304335416bc6, restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y). Bottom line up front: there are four overlapping problems, and the comms only ever discussed a sliver of them. The numbers are worse and broader than the Slack/email traffic suggests, and they do point to us working beyond what's documented.

1) SCOPE IS UNDOCUMENTED — the headline for Matthew
- The ONLY engagement letter on file for Northstar is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11, doc_0036f5b991574808). It's actually the only engagement_letter document in the whole vault.
- The recurring work we're actually doing — monthly bookkeeping/close, IOLTA trust accounting, management-accounts packages, the FY2025 annual accounts, and FY2025 federal + multi-state tax prep (1065/K-1/M-1, the $4,820.30 SALT late-post, the $18,750 ASC-710 partner-draw reclass) — is NOT covered by any engagement letter.
- Independence flag worth raising: we keep Northstar's books AND we're now doing the FY2026 audit (materiality memo 2026-05-18). Bookkeeping + audit on the same client is a self-review question; worth confirming the safeguard, especially given the AR clean-up below.
- We have the mechanism and precedent: the Acme Cloud change order (May) for ~15 hrs of EL overage explicitly referenced "the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement."
(I couldn't pull the EL's full scope text — the stored file is a 102-byte stub — so confirm the exact scope against the signed original.)

2) WIP DOES NOT TIE OUT
- WIP (119000) = $2,013,942.79, ~73% of YTD revenue and growing — a big book of recognised-but-unbilled work, conversion to billings lagging.
- May (FP-2026-05) is still open and past its 6/5 BD5 close; the entire May close is unposted (30 JEs in draft/submitted, none posted). Three WIP-recognition JEs totalling ~$95,289 are sitting unposted:
  • JE-…-0001 $34,495.06 (draft) • JE-…-0020 $45,263.28 (submitted) • JE-…-0051 $15,531.00 (submitted)
- This matches the mid-May Slack thread holding the WIP JE "pending the billable-hours tie-out / client input." So WIP isn't reconciled to confirmed/approved hours. Recommend a WIP→billings reconciliation before we sign off May.

3) AR / ALLOWANCE IS DISTORTED
- Gross AR (110000) = $647,359.49, but Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (117000) = $1,057,727.96 — i.e. a NEGATIVE net receivable — plus $427,018.64 of YTD bad-debt expense.
- An allowance at 164% of gross AR isn't a supportable position; it looks like write-offs expensed without being relieved against the allowance, or an over-provision. Needs clean-up before the FY2026 audit. Day-to-day collection looks fine (monthly realizations and payments both $60–140K); the problem is the allowance/bad-debt clean-up. Precise 30/60/90 buckets need the billing sub-ledger if you want them for the meeting.

4) AP BACKLOG — large and stale
- 87 Northstar vendor invoices sit in pending_approval, ~$302,063 total, NONE with an approver assigned. Aging runs FP-2025-07 → FP-2026-05.
- Oldest: CrownPeak VEN-030-817856 $1,152.41 (invoice 2025-07-08, ~340 days) — mis-coded to 210000, review says re-code to 219000 and release, still pending. Other ~320+ day items: BlueMesa $10,998, LexHarbor $9,864, Ridgewell $8,547, MetroLink $5,634. Material open items also include VaultSpan $12,896, Stonepath $16,214, ArchiveSpring $11,787, Cobalt Harbor $10,867.
- The three CrownPeak items everyone has been emailing about are STILL open in SAP despite being treated as resolved: VEN-030-736427 ($422.32, void approved by Matthew 5/31), VEN-030-353041 ($459.84, dismissed as duplicate), VEN-030-817856 ($1,152.41, re-code & release). None have actually been actioned in SAP.

DOES THE COMMS STORY MATCH THE NUMBERS?
- AP / CrownPeak — NO. Slack/email/Linear say "resolved"; SAP shows all three still pending and is silent on the other 84 invoices (~$300K) and the no-approver routing gap.
- WIP — YES. Comms (hold pending hours tie-out) match the ledger.
- Scope — NO. Comms show heavy accounting/tax/advisory + the audit; the records show only an audit EL. The work has outrun the paperwork.

OPEN TRACKERS
- Linear: 5 open Northstar items, all CrownPeak AP follow-ups (3 in_progress, 2 todo) — all documentation-only; none clear the invoices in SAP.
- Airtable: there was no Northstar engagement record; I've created one in the Close Blocker Triage Log (rec7644df37828c45) reflecting this review.

MY READ FOR THE PARTNER MEETING
The WIP (~$2.0M unbilled) + the breadth of recurring accounting/tax/advisory work, against an audit-only engagement letter, strongly suggests we are delivering beyond documented scope and it has not been captured as a change order. That's the absorb-vs-change-order call for you and Matthew. If change order: paper a current accounting/advisory + tax engagement letter and confirm the audit-independence safeguard. Alongside that: assign an AP approver and clear the ~$302K queue (and actually execute the three CrownPeak dispositions), finish the overdue May close with the WIP tie-out, and clean up the AR allowance before audit fieldwork.

Full detail and figures in the attached working paper.

George

content.text:
Done. Here's where the Northstar engagement actually stands, and what I've put in place.

## What I found (across GL, SAP, Vault, Slack/email, Linear, Airtable)

**1. Scope is undocumented — the headline for Matthew.** The *only* engagement letter on file for Northstar is the **FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter** (executed 2026-05-11). The recurring work we actually do — monthly bookkeeping/close, IOLTA trust accounting, management-accounts packages, FY2025 annual accounts, and FY2025 federal + multi-state tax prep (1065/K-1/M-1, $4,820.30 SALT late-post, $18,750 partner-draw reclass) — is **not covered by any engagement letter**. Plus an independence/self-review flag: we keep the books *and* are now auditing the same entity.

**2. WIP doesn't tie out.** WIP (119000) = **$2.01M (~73% of YTD revenue)**. May (FP-2026-05) is open and past its BD5 close; the whole May close is unposted, including **three WIP-recognition JEs (~$95.3K) blocked pending the billable-hours tie-out/client input** — exactly matching the mid-May Slack thread.

**3. AR/allowance is distorted.** Gross AR = **$647K** but the allowance is **$1.06M** (a *negative* net receivable) with **$427K** YTD bad debt. Not a supportable position — needs clean-up before audit fieldwork.

**4. AP backlog.** **87 invoices, ~$302K, in pending_approval with no approver assigned**, oldest ~340 days (Jul 2025). The three CrownPeak items everyone called "resolved" in the comms are **all still open in SAP** — none voided/recoded/released.

**Comms vs numbers:** WIP matches; **AP and scope do NOT** — the ledger is broader and worse than the Slack/email story (which only ever covered the three CrownPeak invoices).

## What I did
- **Filed** the working paper to Records Vault → `doc_304335416bc6` (kind: memo, **AICPA_SQMS_7Y / restricted**, linked to northstar_legal).
- **Reflected engagement status in Airtable** → created record `rec7644df37828c45` in the Close Blocker Triage Log (owner George McAdam, High, Open).
- **Emailed** the full brief (with the working paper attached) to you and Daniel → email `af411415fa0b4ac5be03b937d9874a0a`.

**The decision for Daniel/Matthew:** absorb, or go for a change order plus a proper accounting/advisory + tax engagement letter — the WIP + AR picture against an audit-only letter is the evidence that we've gone beyond documented scope undocumented.

One caveat: the engagement letter's stored file is a 102-byte stub, so I couldn't read its exact scope text — worth confirming against the signed original. And precise AR aging buckets would need the billing sub-ledger if Daniel wants them in the room.
